pattern: Because all values are positive, growing a window only raises its score, so a two-pointer sweep counts every qualifying subarray in linear time.
hints: With positive numbers, extending a subarray on the right never decreases its sum-times-length score; this monotonicity is the whole trick.
Keep a sliding window whose score stays below k; for each right end, shrink from the left until valid, then all subarrays ending at the right end inside the window qualify.

Maintain a running window sum and a left pointer. For each right index, add nums[right] to the sum; while sum times window length is at least k, remove the left element and advance left. Add (right - left + 1) to the answer.

edge_inputs.description: small array with mixed qualifying subarrays
only the whole array fails the threshold
single element score equals k is excluded
k just above a single element only

stress.description: large array of small positive values with a mid-range k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 3000))
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 11) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(1, 100001)
    return [nums, k]
